title: Deguelia timoriensis
intro: Deguelia timoriensis is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Timor, known for its medicinal properties and use in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Deguelia timoriensis include its potential to inhibit cancer cell growth, exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, and possess antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include timorins, which have been isolated and studied for their pharmacological properties. Deguelia timoriensis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are employed to treat conditions like fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Deguelia timoriensis belongs to the plant family Fabaceae, commonly known as legume or pea family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the Fabaceae family. The Fabaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. Deguelia timoriensis is a member of the subfamily Faboideae, which comprises most of the legume species. The exact classification of Deguelia timoriensis may also include genus and species levels within the Fabaceae family.
